RegSetValueExA.description: Too generic alone; meaningful only in combination with RegCreateKeyExA/RegCreateKeyA or RegOpenKeyExA when writing a persistence value.

RegSetValueExW.description: Unicode variant of RegSetValueExA; meaningful only in combination with RegCreateKeyExW/RegCreateKeyW or RegOpenKeyExW.

RegCreateKeyExA.description: Too generic alone; meaningful only in combination with RegSetValueExA -- creates the key before writing a persistence value.

RegCreateKeyA.description: Legacy variant of RegCreateKeyExA (no security attributes/disposition control); meaningful only in combination with RegSetValueExA.

RegCreateKeyW.description: Unicode legacy variant of RegCreateKeyA; meaningful only in combination with RegSetValueExW.

RegOpenKeyExA.description: Too generic alone; meaningful only in combination with RegSetValueExA -- opens an existing key (e.g. Winlogon) before modifying it.

CoCreateInstance.description: Instantiates a COM object; meaningful only combined with CoInitializeEx. Used for WMI event subscription (IWbemServices), Task Scheduler (ITaskService), or ShellLink creation.

CoInitializeEx.description: COM initialization required before CoCreateInstance; too generic alone.

AddAuthenticationPackage.description: Registers a malicious DLL as an LSA authentication package, loaded into LSASS at boot without requiring a reboot to take effect.

AddSecurityPackage.description: Loads a malicious Security Support Provider DLL into LSASS immediately, without waiting for the next reboot.

CreateServiceA.description: Creates a new Windows service; a common persistence and privilege escalation vector, especially when configured for automatic start.

CreateServiceW.description: Unicode variant of CreateServiceA; creates a new Windows service for persistence.

OpenSCManagerA.description: Opens the service control manager; meaningful only in combination with CreateServiceA to install a new service.

OpenSCManagerW.description: Unicode variant of OpenSCManagerA; meaningful only in combination with CreateServiceW.

StartServiceA.description: Starts a service without waiting for a reboot; meaningful only in combination with CreateServiceA.

StartServiceW.description: Unicode variant of StartServiceA; meaningful only in combination with CreateServiceW.

ChangeServiceConfig2A.description: Modifies service failure actions or description; often used alongside CreateServiceA to make a malicious service blend in or auto-restart.

OpenServiceA.description: Opens a handle to an existing service; meaningful only in combination with ChangeServiceConfigA -- precursor to hijacking a legitimate service.

OpenServiceW.description: Unicode variant of OpenServiceA; meaningful only in combination with ChangeServiceConfigW.

ChangeServiceConfigA.description: Modifies a service's ImagePath (binPath); the actual API used to hijack an existing legitimate service rather than creating a new one.

SHGetFolderPathW.description: Resolves the Startup folder path dynamically (CSIDL_STARTUP) instead of hardcoding it.

SdbCreateDatabase.description: Creates a custom shim database (.sdb) in memory via apphelp.dll, alternative to sdbinst.exe for installing application compatibility shims.

SdbWriteBinaryTag.description: Writes a binary tag (matching rule or patch data) into a shim database; meaningful only in combination with SdbCreateDatabase.

SdbCloseDatabase.description: Finalizes and writes the shim database to disk; meaningful only in combination with SdbCreateDatabase.

RegSetValueExW.description: API name resolved dynamically via GetProcAddress rather than statically imported; meaningful only alongside RegCreateKeyExW/RegCreateKeyW.

RegCreateKeyExW.description: API name resolved dynamically via GetProcAddress; meaningful only alongside RegSetValueExW.

StartServiceW.description: API name resolved dynamically via GetProcAddress; meaningful only alongside CreateServiceW/OpenSCManagerW.

OpenSCManagerW.description: API name resolved dynamically via GetProcAddress; meaningful only alongside CreateServiceW/StartServiceW.

ChangeServiceConfigW.description: API name resolved dynamically via GetProcAddress; used to modify an existing service's ImagePath -- possible service hijack indicator.

HKLM\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\Curr.description: Registry Run key path; programs referenced here execute automatically at logon.

HKCU\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\Curr.description: Per-user Registry Run key path; does not require elevated privileges to write.

HKLM\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\Curr.description: Registry RunOnce key path; executes once at next logon, then self-deletes.

\\Start Menu\\Programs\\Startup.description: Startup folder path; any executable, script, or shortcut placed here runs automatically at logon.

HKLM\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\C.description: Winlogon registry key; modifying its Shell, Userinit, or Notify values hijacks the logon process to launch additional executables.

HKLM\\SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Control.description: LSA Authentication Packages registry value; DLLs listed here are loaded into LSASS at boot for credential interception.
LSA Security Packages registry value; DLLs listed here are loaded as Security Support Providers at boot.
Secondary LSA Security Packages registry path under OSConfig; equally effective for SSP registration.

HKLM\\SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Service.description: Windows Time service providers registry path; a malicious DLL registered here is loaded by the W32Time service at boot.

HKLM\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Active Setup\.description: Active Setup registry path; the StubPath value under a component's GUID key executes once per user at first logon after being set.

00021401-0000-0000-C000-000000000046.description: CLSID of the ShellLink COM object; instantiated via CoCreateInstance to programmatically create or modify .lnk shortcuts.

HKLM\\SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Control.description: AppCertDlls registry key; DLLs listed here are loaded into every process calling CreateProcess, CreateProcessAsUser, or WinExec.

HKLM\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\C.description: AppInit_DLLs registry value; DLLs listed here are loaded by user32.dll into nearly every process on the system.

LoadAppInit_DLLs.description: Registry value that must be set to 1 alongside AppInit_DLLs to activate the loading mechanism.

HKLM\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\C.description: IFEO registry path; a Debugger value set here hijacks execution of the target executable, redirecting it to an arbitrary payload.

SilentProcessExit.description: IFEO-adjacent registry key used with MonitorProcess to launch a payload when the target process silently exits.

Software\\Classes\\CLSID.description: Registry path under which a CLSID's default handler can be hijacked to load an attacker-controlled DLL instead of the legitimate COM object.

InprocServer32.description: Registry subkey holding the DLL path for a COM object; overwriting it redirects any application instantiating that CLSID to a malicious DLL.

__EventFilter.description: WMI class used to define the trigger condition of a permanent event subscription.

__EventConsumer.description: WMI base class for event consumers (e.g. CommandLineEventConsumer, ActiveScriptEventConsumer) that execute a payload when a subscription fires.

CommandLineEventConsumer.description: WMI consumer class that executes an arbitrary command line when its bound event filter triggers -- the most common WMI persistence payload.

__FilterToConsumerBinding.description: WMI class that binds an __EventFilter to an __EventConsumer, completing a permanent event subscription.

root\\subscription.description: WMI namespace where permanent event subscriptions (filters, consumers, bindings) are stored.

HKLM\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\C.description: Registry path caching registered scheduled task metadata; direct writes here can register a task without going through the public Task Scheduler API.

\\Windows\\System32\\Tasks\\.description: Filesystem path where scheduled task XML definitions are stored.

schtasks.exe.description: Built-in command-line utility for creating and managing scheduled tasks; commonly invoked via CreateProcess or ShellExecute rather than linked directly.

bitsadmin.exe.description: Built-in command-line utility for managing BITS jobs; can be used to download and execute a payload or establish a persistent job.

4991d34b-80a1-4291-83b6-3328366b9097.description: CLSID of the BackgroundCopyManager COM object; instantiated to create and manage BITS jobs programmatically instead of via bitsadmin.exe.

ActiveScriptEventConsumer.description: WMI consumer class that executes VBScript or JScript when its bound event filter triggers -- an alternative to CommandLineEventConsumer.

NTEventLogEventConsumer.description: WMI consumer class that writes to the NT event log when triggered; occasionally abused as a low-noise persistence payload.

sdbinst.exe.description: Built-in utility to install a custom shim database (.sdb).

HKLM\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\C.description: Registry key associating a custom shim GUID with a target executable.
Registry key listing installed custom shim databases on the system.

__COMPAT_LAYER.description: Environment variable used to apply a compatibility layer without installing a full shim database.

shell\\open\\command.description: Registry key suffix written under a ProgID to redirect execution of a file extension to an arbitrary handler.

\\UserChoice.description: Subkey under FileExts\<ext> storing the chosen handler for a file extension; the modern Explorer-based association mechanism.

FileExts.description: Too generic alone; meaningful only in combination with \UserChoice for file association hijacking.

591209C7-767B-42B2-9FBA-44EE4615F2C7.description: CLSID of IApplicationAssociationRegistration; instantiated via CoCreateInstance to change a file or protocol association through COM instead of direct registry writes.

combinations.name: Registry Run Key persistence
combinations.description: Creates and writes to a registry key -- likely installing a Run/RunOnce autostart entry.

combinations.name: Registry Run Key persistence (Unicode)
combinations.description: Unicode variant: creates and writes to a registry key -- likely installing a Run/RunOnce autostart entry.

combinations.name: Registry Run Key persistence (legacy API)
combinations.description: Uses the legacy RegCreateKeyA (no Ex) alongside RegSetValueExA -- same registry persistence pattern via the older API.

combinations.name: Winlogon Helper DLL persistence
combinations.description: Opens and writes to an existing registry key -- likely modifying Winlogon's Shell, Userinit, or Notify values.

combinations.name: Winlogon Helper DLL persistence (Unicode)
combinations.description: Unicode variant of Winlogon Helper DLL persistence.

combinations.name: Windows service creation and start
combinations.description: Creates a new service and starts it immediately without waiting for a reboot -- classic service-based persistence.

combinations.name: Windows service creation and start (Unicode)
combinations.description: Unicode variant of service-based persistence: creates a new service and starts it immediately.

combinations.name: COM-based persistence mechanism
combinations.description: Initializes COM and instantiates an object -- required for WMI event subscription, Task Scheduler registration, or ShellLink-based shortcut creation. Confirm the specific mechanism via accompanying strings (WMI class names, task registry paths, or the ShellLink CLSID).

combinations.name: Existing service hijack via reconfiguration
combinations.description: Opens an existing service and rewrites its ImagePath -- hijacks a legitimate service instead of creating a new one, evading service-creation-based detection.

combinations.name: Programmatic shim database creation
combinations.description: Builds a malicious shim database via API calls rather than dropping a pre-built .sdb and invoking sdbinst.exe -- stealthier, avoids the sdbinst.exe string.
